name: Green-Tao Theorem
description: Green-Tao Theorem (2004): The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions. For every k ≥ 3, there exist infinitely many k-term arithmetic progressions of primes.
mermaid:
graph TD
    ThmSz["Thm: Szemerédi\nDense sets contain k-AP"]
    DefPseudo["Def: Pseudorandom measure ν\nMajorant for primes"]
    DefLinear["Def: (k-1)-linear forms\nMultilinear structure"]
    DefDense["Def: Dense model\nSparse set approximable by dense"]
    L1["Lemma: Transference principle\nDense → primes via majorant"]
    L2["Lemma: W-trick\nRestrict to n ≡ b mod W"]
    L3["Lemma: Goldston-Yıldırım\nCorrelation condition for ν"]
    L4["Lemma: Dense model theorem\nSparse ≈ dense in Gowers norm"]
    ThmGT["Thm: Green-Tao 2004\nPrimes contain k-AP ∀k"]
    DefUnif["Def: Gowers uniformity on primes\nU^k control"]
    ThmSz --> L1
    DefPseudo --> L1
    DefDense --> L1
    L1 --> ThmGT
    L2 --> DefPseudo
    L3 --> DefPseudo
    L4 --> L1
    DefLinear --> L3
    DefUnif --> L4
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef lemma fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    class DefPseudo,DefLinear,DefDense,DefUnif definition
    class ThmSz,ThmGT theorem
    class L1,L2,L3,L4 lemma
